entry.title: Re: How can we Approve the PO in program like we do in My...
entry.content: in BAPI_PO_RELEASE, there is a mandatory importing parameter [PO_REL_CODE],which is for PO Rlease Strategy. Since we are not using PO Rlease Strategy, so all POs are having empty value for this field. Even when I try to call this BAPI with empty value in this field, it returns with error saying that I don't have authorization. But actually I do have the authorization to approve PO in My Inbox app.

entry.title: Re: Is there a Tcode to extract the list of corresponding...
entry.content:
Hello @20252022 

You can access required information with the outbound delivery monitor, VL06O transaction. Use selection criteria to get deliveries you need, then go to items view and filter out by "SD Document Category" field = V, which will get you purchase order / STO related deliveries. Corresponding STO numbers are presented in the "Ref. Doc." field. The items view gives you SKU details, i.e. material number, description, quantity etc.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Conversion to S/4HANA: Issue with custom long texts and concatenated TDNAME including MATNR
entry.content:
No worries, @VXLozano!

From what I've seen, we don't have any issues with the creation of new long texts (standard or custom) and then reading those again in other processes because the "formatting" of TDNAME is fine using 40 characters for the MATNR during creation and reading.

What we do have issues with, are the many already existing entries in STXH where TDNAME was filled in ECC when the MATNR was just 18 characters long and VKORG followed at position 19, but when the text is read in S/4HANA, SAP builds TDNAME with 40 characters for MATNR expecting the VKORG to be at position 41 and then obviously doesn't find the entries. This text selection is happening in SAP standard code during material maintenance which we cannot control.

For SAP-standard texts, the content in STXH-TDNAME is changed during the system conversion but for our custom long texts this conversion doesn't happen.

Does that help to clarify our issue?

Cheers

Bärbel

entry.title: SAP Business One Down Payment Reconciliation Error
entry.content:
Hello,

My client was posted a Down Payment Request with an amount of 23000 INR. And they paid for that downpayment amount with Outgoing Payment module. They create a A/P invoice for that vendor worth 15300 INR and also reconcile that Downpayment amount 15300 with that invoice amount. Balance 7700 INR was balance in Down Payment. That vendor paid that balance amount 7700 INR. Client was entered that amount directly in Incoming payment. Now they try to reconcile that Downpayment 7700 with Incoming Payment 7700 but it shows error 'A regular payment cannot be reconciled with a payment from a down payment request [Message 3821-45]'. Now what can i do. Pls anyone help me to close this issue asap.

entry.title: SAP Signavio May 2026 Product Release - Process Transformation Management and Collaboration
entry.content:
SAP Signavio May 2026 Product Release - Process Transformation Management and Collaboration

This one is an exciting release for us and I’m excited to share with you the innovations coming with our major release for May 2026. Some of you may have already heard this in Orlando last week at SAP Sapphire and this release continues our focus on helping organizations drive transformation through intelligent processes, real business value, and one connected suite for all.

And specifically for process transformation and collaboration, we are introducing new ways for you to capture transformation value more flexibly and receive tailored initiative recommendations to strengthen continuous value practices and business steering across the organization. As well as a new customizable home experience that brings together the most relevant information and tools together in one place.

Let’s get started!

 

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager

Flexibly capture value in various forms

Transformation value comes in many forms, from measurable financial impact to operational and strategic improvements. To realize that value effectively, you need a consistent way to capture, structure, and manage it across transformation initiatives.

With this release, SAP Signavio Process Transformation Manager introduces a more flexible way to capture and manage value directly within transformation initiatives. Organizations can now capture:

  • Performance metrics in flexible units
  • Additional potential monetary value
  • Explicit financial classifications such as profit & loss or working capital

These manual value cases are treated as first-class citizens and aggregated into existing financial summaries, ensuring consistency with value cases in our process analysis and mining solution. The result is a more flexible and scalable way to capture transformation value without compromising financial semantics or reporting consistency.

 

AI-Assisted Transformation Advisory, Initiative Builder for Value Practice and Business Steering​

For most organizations, the hardest part of transformation isn't identifying value, it's establishing the right continuous practices, securing business sponsorship, and finding the organizational setup that allows transformation to stick. Process data alone can't surface these gaps.

This new Initiative Builder for Value Practice and Business Steering capability extends our existing AI-Assisted Transformation Advisory's Initiative Builder capability to beyond process optimization alone. By assessing your organization’s current transformation maturity based on value practices and business steering, it recommends tailored initiatives designed to close gaps, strengthen execution, and improve transformation outcomes.

Each recommended initiative comes prefilled with the guidance teams need to take action immediately. This includes relevant insights, suggested actions, execution tasks, and supporting assets. Instead of starting from a blank page, organizations receive a clear steering framework that guides them from value identification through to value realization.

Designed to be practical and easy to use, our new capability replaces lengthy discovery exercises with focused prompts that quickly generate recommendations tailored to an organization’s maturity and priorities. The result is a more continuous and sustainable approach to transformation, helping organizations establish stronger value practices, improve business steering, and build transformation as an ongoing capability rather than a one-time project.

 

SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub

Customizable home experience for advanced users

With this release, SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub introduces a more personalized start experience that brings the most relevant information and tools together in one place. Designed to help users stay focused and work more efficiently, the experience makes it easier to access what matters most and navigate directly to key tasks and insights.

Users can tailor their starting view by selecting the content elements most relevant to their role, responsibilities, and priorities. This helps individuals quickly access important information, reduce unnecessary navigation, and stay focused on current work and objectives. For more information on this feature please check out this blog.

 

Want to know what else is coming out as part of this release?

Check out our highlight blog for SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite and find out what else we have prepared!

entry.title: SAP Signavio value accelerators: what’s new in the May 2026 release
entry.content:
Accelerating enterprise transformation today demands more than visibility alone: it requires precise insights that translate directly into action. This latest set of SAP Signavio value accelerators builds on that need by you detect inefficiencies, reduce complexity, and improve execution across finance, supply chain, manufacturing, procurement, and warehouse operations. By combining targeted improvement dashboards with concrete, system specific recommendations, these accelerators make it easier to move from observation to optimization across critical SAP processes. 

Spanning topics from inventory health, warehouse performance, and transportation data quality to contract management, payment terms, asset accounting, and make‑to‑order delivery reliability, this release equips you to address both operational friction and structural weaknesses. Whether the goal is to increase automation, clean up unused or obsolete data, improve on‑time delivery, strengthen financial performance, or unlock working capital potential, SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and Process Insights provide the clarity and focus needed to prioritize the right actions. Together, these value accelerators help teams drive measurable improvements faster, supporting more reliable execution, better cross functional alignment, and sustained business value at scale. 

Read on to explore what’s new for this May release: 

 

Value Accelerator Library 

Selective installation of Value Accelerator content to SAP Signavio Process Manager 

Built for transformation professionals such as enterprise architects, process consultants, and BPM leads who need to work efficiently with SAP best practice content, this upcoming release introduces selective import capabilities for value accelerators targeting SAP Signavio Process Manager. It enables teams to search, select, and install only the models and assets that are relevant to their specific industry, process variants, or workshop needs, rather than deploying a full content package.  

By supporting an explore‑to‑consume experience with just a few clicks, this feature shortens installation times, reduces workspace clutter, and allows follow‑up activities, such as Fit‑to‑Standard workshops or process landscape design, to start immediately. With clear visibility into selected and installed content, teams can build a focused, high-quality process landscape faster, prioritize what truly matters, and accelerate transformation activities without the overhead of managing unnecessary content. 

 

Cross-LoB 

Analyze Agent Execution Performance in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence 

Built for process owners, IT leaders, and compliance stakeholders looking to safely scale the use of AI agents, the Analyze Agent Execution Performance value accelerator for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence introduces a foundational governance and monitoring layer for agent‑driven processes. By transforming raw agent execution logs into process mining ready event data, it delivers comprehensive dashboards that make agent usage, behavior patterns, and outcomes fully transparent.  

This accelerator enables teams to monitor agent‑led process execution, detect deviations, risks, and inefficiencies, and systematically identify high‑impact improvement opportunities related to agent orchestration. As a result, organizations gain greater trust, auditability, and control over agent adoption, mitigating risk, unlocking process efficiency, and confidently embedding AI agents into critical business processes while maintaining performance, compliance, and accountability. 

 

Supply Chain 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to reduce old and obsolete transactional data in supply chain (SAP Transportation Management) 

Designed for supply chain and SAP Transportation Management professionals responsible for maintaining efficient, scalable operations, this value accelerator delivers a dedicated dashboard to reduce old and obsolete transactional data. By providing clear transparency into document backlogs such as freight orders, freight units, bookings, transportation units, and settlement documents, it helps teams pinpoint where high volumes indicate that standard automatic closing processes are not working as intended. With actionable improvement insights and correction recommendations, including guidance on how to mass close outdated documents that are not yet achievable, this accelerator enables organizations to clean up transactional backlogs, restore process efficiency, and ensure a clean transactional landscape by reducing backlog volumes and improving automatic document closure, supporting stable operations and future optimization. 

 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to remove data objects with no transactional usage in supply chain (SAP Extended Warehouse Management) 

Built for supply chain and warehouse optimization teams aiming to streamline their SAP Extended Warehouse Management systems, this capability for SAP Signavio Process Insights within SAP Signavio Process Intelligence supports all improvement opportunity dashboards, providing clear visibility into customizing and master data that show no transactional usage. Through a focused improvement dashboard, it surfaces underutilized or unused configuration elements such as warehouse task process types, storage types, empty storage bins, and warehouse order creation rules that may be driving unnecessary complexity.  

By translating usage insights into concrete cleanup recommendations, the accelerator helps teams assess what is truly needed, safely remove redundant objects, and streamline warehouse processes. The result is a leaner, more manageable warehouse system that is easier to operate, maintain, and evolve as business requirements change.

 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to remove data objects with no transactional usage in supply chains (SAP Transportation Management) 

Designed for transportation experts looking to streamline their Transportation Management system, this value accelerator delivers a focused dashboard that uncovers unused customizing and master data within SAP Transportation Management. It brings transparency into how freight agreements are applied in freight orders and bookings, how location IDs are used across transportation steps, and whether configured document types are actively leveraged across different transportation order scenarios.  

By pairing these usage insights with clear correction recommendations, the accelerator supports informed decisions on which data objects can be safely retired. This helps teams reduce system complexity, improve data quality, and maintain a cleaner, more efficient SAP Transportation Management environment that better supports standardized processes and future optimization efforts. 

 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to reduce slow moving stock (EWM managed) 

Developed for warehouse and inventory management teams seeking to reduce excess stock and improve space utilization, this value accelerator provides a clear view of inventory that has not moved for a defined period and should be reviewed for potential reduction. Through a focused dashboard, it highlights unrestricted use stock with no movement for at least 30 days, quality inspection stock inactive for seven days, and nonempty storage bins without recent activity.  

By combining transparency into slow‑moving and dormant inventory with practical correction recommendations, this accelerator helps teams identify products requiring closer review, initiate timely corrective measures, and optimize warehouse operations, supporting healthier inventory levels and more efficient use of storage capacity. 

 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to reduce warehouse management internal cost 

This value accelerator delivers a dedicated dashboard that highlights manual interventions and reprocessing activities in SAP Extended Warehouse Management, focused on helping warehouse operations teams identify and eliminate avoidable manual work. It brings transparency into areas such as warehouse task cancellations, exception handling via processing codes, and manually assigned warehouse orders across inbound and outbound processes, revealing where standard execution is breaking down.  

By quantifying cancellation rates, exception driven task processing, and manual order handling, the accelerator enables teams to pinpoint inefficiencies, reduce recurring exceptions, and increase the level of automation over time. With clear insights into where corrective actions are needed, organizations can streamline warehouse execution, lower operational effort, and move closer to stable, standardized processes. 

 

Identify Improvement potential for embedded SAP Extended Warehouse Management (Outbound) 

Designed for warehouse process owners seeking a clear entry point to improve outbound performance, this value accelerator within SAP Signavio Process Intelligence helps uncover untapped value and optimization opportunities across warehouse operations. It provides a consolidated view of key performance indicators and process variants, enabling continuous monitoring and transparency into how warehouses operate.  

By highlighting primary pain points, inefficiencies, and rework drivers, the accelerator supports targeted deep dives into areas with the greatest improvement and savings potential. With prioritized insights into where cycle times, handling effort, and costs can be reduced, organizations can systematically improve warehouse efficiency, boost on-time delivery rates, and strengthen trust and collaboration with customers and downstream stakeholders. This value accelerators package incorporates the data transformation pipeline, which is required by analytical EWM dashboard value accelerator packages, such as “Improve process performance for SAP Extended Warehouse Management (Outbound)”.

 

Improve process performance for SAP Extended Warehouse Management (Outbound) 

Built for warehouse process owners aiming to lower warehousing costs and improve on‑time delivery performance, this value accelerator within SAP Signavio Process Intelligence helps uncover the root causes behind delayed customer order fulfillment. It provides end‑to‑end visibility into outbound warehouse execution, from sales order creation through goods issue, highlighting delivery reliability, proactive risk signals, and critical performance gaps. By enabling cross‑warehouse benchmarking and detailed process variant analysis, the accelerator allows teams to identify best practices, spot outliers, and understand where rework in picking, packing, or staging activities are driving delays and missed delivery targets. With deep insights into bottlenecks, exceptions, cancellations, and delivery blocks, organizations can focus improvement efforts where they matter most, reduce handling effort, shorten cycle times, and consistently increase on‑time delivery rates for customers. This value accelerator package contains an extension dashboard for the EWM value accelerator package “Identify Improvement potential for embedded SAP Extended Warehouse Management (Outbound)”. 

 

Sales 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to improve on-time delivery performance in make to order 

Rather than treating on‑time delivery issues as isolated symptoms, this value accelerator gives sales and manufacturing teams a shared, data‑driven view of where delays originate in make‑to‑order scenarios. The Improvement Opportunity dashboard in SAP Signavio Process Insights reveals how frequently customer deliveries miss their committed dates and pinpoints whether delays stem from late manufacturing completion, delayed goods issue, or misaligned confirmations between sales and production.  

With detailed visibility into late goods issues, overdue confirmed delivery dates, and shifted customer commitments, the accelerator makes gaps between planning and execution immediately tangible. Paired with targeted correction recommendations, it supports better synchronization between sales and manufacturing, helps align confirmation and scheduling dates, and enables teams to address bottlenecks before they impact the customer, ultimately driving more reliable deliveries and higher customer satisfaction. 

 

Finance 

Improvement opportunity dashboard to reduce days to close books in asset accounting 

Instead of spending valuable time resolving avoidable errors during period end close, this value accelerator helps asset accounting teams proactively identify and address the root causes behind manual rework. The Improvement Opportunity dashboard in SAP Signavio Process Insights sheds light on issues such as failed depreciation runs, errors in periodic postings, incomplete asset master data, and low value assets that remain active longer than expected.  

By making these problem areas visible, before and during closing activities, the accelerator enables teams to understand where unplanned depreciations occur and why downstream processing is blocked. Supported by targeted correction recommendations, it guides users in maintaining missing master data and resolving errors early, helping reduce days to close, minimize manual interventions, and achieve a smoother, more predictable period‑end closing process in asset accounting. 

 

Delay Unfavorable Supplier Payments by Using Optimal Payment Terms dashboard for invoice to pay in SAP ERP Central Component and SAP S/4HANA 

Rather than reacting to payment‑term issues only after invoices are posted, this value accelerator gives finance teams a structured way to analyze and optimize supplier payment behavior across the invoice‑to‑pay process. The Delay Unfavorable Supplier Payments dashboard in SAP Signavio Process Intelligence brings together master data, purchase orders, and financial invoices to make inconsistencies in payment terms immediately visible and traceable to their root causes.  

By highlighting immediate and unfavorable terms, flagging high‑risk cases that require review, and exposing where agreed conditions are not being applied correctly, the dashboard directs attention to the areas with the greatest working‑capital impact. With clear improvement insights and defined actions, organizations can extend days payable outstanding, reduce manual intervention, improve compliance with negotiated terms, and ultimately strengthen both financial performance and supplier relationships. 

 

Procurement 

Improve contracting process performance in SAP Ariba 

Rather than viewing contract management as a black box within Source‑to‑Contract, this value accelerator gives contract managers a clear, KPI‑driven view into how contracts are created, used, and governed in SAP Ariba. This value accelerator for SAP Signavio Process Intelligence builds on the existing Source‑to‑Pay performance insights by adding a dedicated focus on contract management. It consolidates contract related data into an intuitive overview and specialized deep dive dashboards, making it easier to understand utilization levels, execution quality, and continuity risks. 

By exposing gaps in contract usage, price integrity issues, and expiring or missing contracts, the accelerator helps organizations increase contracted spend coverage, realize more negotiated benefits, and reduce compliance and continuity risks. At the same time, visibility into process cycle times and optimization potential supports faster, more efficient contract creation, enabling procurement teams to drive higher productivity and stronger value from their contract portfolio. 

 

Conclusion 

This latest set of SAP Signavio value accelerators further deepens how organizations can analyze, improve, and continuously optimize their business processes across finance, procurement, sales, manufacturing, and supply chain operations. By expanding the portfolio of improvement opportunity dashboards and performance‑driven insights, SAP Signavio continues to help teams move beyond high‑level visibility toward concrete, data‑backed actions that reduce complexity, increase automation, and improve execution reliability in their SAP landscapes. 

By uniting SAP best‑practice knowledge with powerful analytical capabilities from SAP Signavio Process Intelligence and Process Insights, these value accelerators enable transformation leaders and process owners to better align strategic objectives with day‑to‑day operations. From improving on‑time delivery and working‑capital performance to simplifying master data, streamlining warehouse execution, and strengthening contract and payment compliance, the focus remains on driving measurable outcomes where they matter most. 

To check out all the new accelerators mentioned in this blog post, access the value accelerator library which is embedded into SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite. For all SAP Signavio Process Insights value accelerators, you can access them directly through the dashboard, Find the value accelerator library in the side panel of SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub and get started today. 

If you’re interested in learning more about the rest of the SAP Signavio solutions, check out the blog posts below:  

 

  • SAP Signavio May 2026 Release Highlights   
  • Process Analysis and Mining   
  • Process and Journey Modeling 
  • New AI capabilities and enhancements   
  • Process Transformation Management and Collaboration   
  • Process Governance

entry.title: SAP Signavio Process and Journey Modeling: what's new in the May 2026 release
entry.content:
SAP Signavio continues to enhance how teams' model, govern, and connect processes across the enterprise with this latest release, strengthening usability, consistency, and integration across the process lifecycle. The updates featured in this release help you move more efficiently from design to execution by simplifying model migration, improving traceability, and ensuring reliable synchronization between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM. 

With expanded import options for decision models, improved visibility across process variants and templates, and tighter alignment between modeling, requirements, and execution environments, this release further reduces manual effort and operational complexity. Together, these capabilities improve modeling accuracy, support cleaner governance, and foster stronger collaboration across business and IT. By connecting processes, decisions, and requirements into a cohesive landscape, SAP Signavio empowers you to model and manage change with greater confidence and control, laying a solid foundation for scalable, enterprise‑wide transformation. 

Here is a summary of what the new Mary release brings to you:

 

Supporting data migration from other systems 

Ensure seamless integration between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM by intelligently verifying whether a process model already exists before synchronization. This feature checks if a SAP Signavio process model is already present in SAP Cloud ALM, whether it originated from SAP Signavio, SAP Solution Manager, or another connected application lifecycle management system, and then automatically determines the correct action: updating the existing solution process flow diagram or creating a new one when needed.  

By managing unique external reference links for every solution process, including creating them on first update when missing, it preserves a single, authoritative version of each process in SAP Cloud ALM. As a result, you can confidently sync process models without risking duplication, knowing that SAP Cloud ALM always reflects a consistent, unique solution process, regardless of its source, while strengthening governance and traceability across the transformation landscape. 

 

Enable SAP Cloud ALM Requirements in SAP Signavio 

Enable tighter alignment between requirements management and process design with this capability that brings SAP Cloud ALM requirements directly into the SAP Signavio Collaboration Hub. When enabled by a workspace administrator, a new Requirements section becomes available on the diagram page, allowing users to view all requirements assigned to an SAP Signavio process model, clearly grouped by project and scope.  

From within SAP Signavio, you can navigate seamlessly to SAP Cloud ALM to create new requirements, review detailed requirement information, or update existing ones, while maintaining flexibility to opt out if needed. By providing centralized visibility, contextual linking, and a detailed requirement panel per entry, this feature strengthens collaboration between business and IT, ensures requirements stay connected to the processes they impact, and supports more consistent, end‑to‑end governance across transformation initiatives. 

Watch the demo 

 

XML Import for decision diagrams 

Expand decision model migration flexibility by enabling XML import for decision diagrams within SAP Signavio. This feature allows you to bring decision models from a much broader range of external tools beyond the previous SGX file limitation directly into the SAP Signavio Business Transformation Suite.  

By supporting XML‑based imports, teams can migrate existing decision diagrams faster, work with a wider ecosystem of decision modeling tools, and avoid unnecessary re‑work during transitions. The result is a smoother migration experience, greater tool compatibility, and more flexible import options that help you onboard decision content efficiently and continue their modeling activities without disruption. 

 

Addition of variants and templates to the dictionary’s “Used in” field 

Surface complete usage visibility for dimension values by displaying references to process variants and templates directly in the dictionary’s “Used in” field. This enhancement extends familiar dictionary navigation beyond standard process diagrams, allowing users to instantly see where specific entries are applied across variant management configurations. By eliminating the need to manually inspect individual variants, you can more efficiently manage and validate complex variant landscapes. The result is faster impact analysis, reduced maintenance effort, and greater confidence when updating or reusing dictionary entries across process variants and templates. 

 

SAP Signavio in Academia: What’s Changing and What’s Next 

SAP Signavio is deepening its commitment to process transformation education by evolving its academic offering. With broader access to SAP Signavio’s full capabilities, more flexible access models, and a roadmap aligned with real-world enterprise practice, universities, faculty, and students are set to benefit from a stronger, future-ready setup. 

As part of this evolution, SAP Signavio has shared an important early update: the current self-service SAP Signavio Academic Edition will be retired in 2026. This change makes way for a modernized academic offering that includes the full SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite, covering not only modeling and collaboration, but also advanced capabilities such as SAP Signavio Process Intelligence. 

What this means for universities and learners 

  • Existing academic users can continue working with the current Academic Edition until September 30, 2026, without disruption. 
  • Ahead of retirement, institutions and learners will be guided on how to export and retain coursework, models, and assignments. 

Going forward, universities will be able to choose from three complementary access models, ranging from free, university-managed access to fully guided academic experiences via trusted partners, as well as individual sandbox environments for students. 

This phased transition is designed to give academia more choice, more capability, and more alignment with how organizations actually use SAP Signavio today, while ensuring continuity for ongoing teaching and research.  This dedicated blog post explains the timeline in detail, outlines each access model, and provides clear guidance on what lecturers and students should do next. Read the full update here to explore your options and prepare for the transition.   

Questions or ready to explore your options? 
 signavio.academics@sap.com 

 

The new and upcoming SAP Signavio Process Modeler enters Beta release 

SAP Signavio is launching a completely redesigned Process Modeler with AI-first architecture, modern UX and deep suite integration. AI-assisted functionality supports intelligent decision-making and cross-team collaborative business design, enabling organizations to turn ideas into operational processes. Redesigned features include real-time collaborative process modelling, intuitive design, AI-powered text-to-process and image-to-process creation, conversational process modelling, and intelligent suggestions.  

Connected to live business data and the full SAP Signavio Transformation suite, Process Modeler enables end-to-end process management across modeling, execution, analytics and governance for scalable, continuously optimized processes that evolve with their business needs. This new product is planned to have it’s General Availability by December 2026, so keep an eye out for updates! 

 

SAP Signavio Process Governance

Workflow action: SAP LeanIX application lifecycle status in the workflow

Diagrams often reference applications that are approaching end of life or nearing phase-out. Without visibility into this risk, approvals can move forward based on outdated or soon-to-expire applications, increasing exposure and creating rework later in the transformation lifecycle.

With this action, approval workflows can now automatically identify and flag applications that are expired or close to expiry directly within the approval process. This helps reviewers make more informed decisions early, avoid unnecessary approvals, and reduce downstream risk. By leveraging the existing SAP LeanIX integration, the capability adds lifecycle transparency to approvals with minimal additional overhead.

Workflow action: Microsoft SharePoint Item Renaming on Move

This new action allows process modelers to rename items directly during move operations, simplifying content organization and reducing manual effort within governance workflows. By enabling inline renaming for Microsoft SharePoint content, organizations can avoid duplicate-name conflicts in target locations, reduce the risk of failed move operations, and streamline content management across SharePoint folders. The result is a smoother and more efficient governance experience with fewer manual steps and improved content organization.

 

Conclusions 

This release reinforces SAP Signavio’s commitment to making process modeling, integration, and governance more intuitive, reliable, and scalable across the enterprise. By enabling flexible migration from external modeling and decision tools, strengthening traceability across variants and templates, and deepening synchronization with SAP Cloud ALM, the new capabilities help you reduce manual effort while increasing consistency and oversight. 

Together, these enhancements support a more connected and trustworthy process landscape, one that simplifies collaboration between business and IT, aligns processes with requirements and execution, and makes it easier to manage complexity over time. As organizations continue to navigate ongoing transformation, SAP Signavio provides the structure, transparency, and integration needed to drive continuous improvement and sustain transformation at scale.

If you’re interested in learning more about the rest of the new features of the SAP Signavio May release, check out the blog posts below:   

  • SAP Signavio May 2026 Release Highlights   
  • Process Analysis and Mining   
  • Value Accelerators 
  • New AI capabilities and enhancements   
  • Process Transformation Management and Collaboration

entry.title: SAP Signavio May 2026 Release: Unlock Next‑Gen Process Intelligence with the power of AI
entry.content:
Sustainable transformation demands more than gut feeling. But too many businesses still rely on incomplete data, siloed views, and assumptions when making their most critical improvement decisions. The next generation of SAP Signavio Process Intelligence is here to change that. It gives you full transparency into how your processes truly run, where performance falls short, and what to prioritize first. In this release, the new generation of Process Intelligence introduces innovative capabilities like Process Networks, Process Atoms, and AI-based capabilities , among others, that don't just surface insights, they guide your next best action, accelerate analysis, and make advanced process mining accessible to every user, regardless of expertise. From first insight to full transformation, you now have everything you need to move faster, decide smarter, and drive meaningful business outcomes. 

One Product, Three Ways to Start — Find the Right Fit for Your Journey 

Every transformation journey is different. Whether you're just exploring what's possible, looking for instant out-of-the-box insights, or ready to run deep-dive analysis across your entire operation — SAP Signavio Process Intelligence has the right starting point for you. And as your needs evolve, moving from one tier to the next is seamless, so you can always go deeper without starting over. 

We have structured SAP Signavio Process Intelligence into three clear entry points. 

Discovery Capabilities  

A trial experience, risk-free, designed to let you validate value quickly and see what's possible before any commercial decision. Try it, prove it, upgrade when ready. No pressure. 

Base Capabilities, out-of-the-box 

Get a set of instant, out-of-the-box insights for your SAP systems. They are ready to use from day one, lightweight by design, with the ability to dive deeper into advanced process intelligence when required. This option strikes the right balance between speed and depth. 

Premium Capabilities, advanced analysis 

Get one experience with all capabilities, both base and premium. Leverage instant insights when you need a quick answer, and deep analysis and extensive process mining capabilities when you need to understand the root cause.  

See It in Action — Innovative Capabilities That Drive Real Results 

Below you will find a list of the innovative features available with Process Intelligence from May on. 

Easy Conformance Checking via Process Atoms 

Leverage reusable process atoms to check if processes are running as designed, allowing you to define and validate process conformance rules without coding. Spotting compliance breaches and inefficiencies also becomes more straightforward as conformance rules can be updated as business needs change, without needing to completely rebuild your BPMN models.  

 

End to end visibility via Process Networks, discovery 

Gain clear visibility into how orders, invoices, deliveries, and other business objects interact across processes. Without changing or duplicating your data, focus on the objects and questions which matter most and understand how everything connects. With an understanding of how your business really works, making adjustments, changing business priorities, and making business decisions becomes easier.  

 

Instant customer feedback via AI-assisted Context Analyzer  

Text-to-event matching  

See customer feedback directly in the context of your processes, where free-text customer feedback connects to process events, and you can identify improvement opportunities quicker with AI-driven insights. This also brings operational and experience data together in one view, eliminating manual data mapping and complex data preparation.  

Sentiment analysis 

Instantly understand positive, negative, and neutral feedback at scale, where free-text customer feedback is analyzed to identify sentiment and customer experience. These AI-driven insights can then be used to decide where and how to enhance your process performance and customer satisfaction.  

Quickly remedy processes via AI-assisted Root Cause Analysis  

Identify the root causes of your performance issues, then receive clear insights and prioritized suggestions to remedy your process. By understanding the why behind your business issues with interpreted analysis and clear explanations, you can act with more confidence and focus on changes that deliver the greatest business value in a shorter time frame. 

 

Test process outcomes via Data-Driven Business Process Simulation  

Simulate and test process changes in a virtual environment using real data, allowing you to evaluate “what-if” scenarios and predict their impact before implementation. This capability can be leveraged for risk-free decision making as you can test outcomes of changes without impacting live operations. This also allows you to avoid costly implementation when mistakes occur and plan better resource allocation.  

This capability will be available in open beta in July.

Want to know more about this release? Check out the highlight blog post and see what else we prepared for this release over the entire SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite!

We are looking forward to seeing you on the SAP Signavio Community to share feedback, questions and discuss with your peers! This is your space!  

Please feel free to share your questions around the release or feedback in the comments with us. You can also reach out to us via the SAP Signavio Community or alternatively watch our Release Webcast on Demand.

entry.title: Hybrid Specification Management Under the Hood
entry.content:
Setting Up the Connection

First step is to implement two SAP Notes on your SAP S/4HANA system (release 2021 or above), as follows:

  • SAP Note 3653636 version 2 or higher
  • SAP Note 3695812 version 3 or higher

There is one more thing to do before the actual synchronization, and that is to set up the cloud connector from SAP IPD to your SAP S/4HANA system in your BTP cockpit. For this, there are detailed instructions available on the Help Portal. You also have to have the ConfigurationPackageActivator role assigned to you, because this role allows you to perform the synchronization of property configurations between the systems.

After all of these prerequisites are done, you are ready to synchronize the property configurations from your S/4HANA system to your SAP IPD system.

Synchronization between Systems

Please bear in mind that the intended use for the hybrid specification management is that the leading system is the S/4HANA system, therefore any changes in the property configurations should be done there and synchronized to your IPD system. What’s more, the best is to have your SAP IPD specification management database empty before the synchronization, because if there are for example conflicting IDs, the mapping of the properties can’t happen.

Synchronization itself is always based on property trees. The reason behind this is that most probably your company doesn’t want to synchronize all items from your S/4HANA system, only the ones that will take part in supplier collaboration or other activities provided by SAP IPD.

Therefore the first decision point is: which property trees and underlying properties you would like to synchronize? When the property trees are decided, the next question is: what type of properties and fields does it contain?

What to Check in your SAP S/4HANA System

Before synchronization, review the value assignment types within your property tree. Here is a little help with that.

The hybrid specification management scenario supports the following types of value assignment types:

  • Type A – these will be modelled as attribute properties on the SAP IPD side
  • Type B, C, RMS-D and RMS-N – these will be modelled as composition properties on the SAP IPD side

VAT Type A

For type A value assignment types, only the following characteristics are supported:

  • Character based
  • Phrase enabled character based
  • Numeric
  • Date

Please consider when synchronizing, that time and currency type characteristic are not taken over currently, and also multivalue characteristics are not supported. This means that the mentioned property fields are excluded from synchronization.

For phrase enabled fields, please make sure that there is a phrase set assigned to the respective characteristic. The synchronization takes phrase sets into consideration and only these phrases included in the chosen property’s assigned phrase sets will be created on SAP IPD side.

VAT Type B and C

For B and C type composition properties, namely listings and standard composition, the values that can be added to the composition are specifications on the S/4HANA side. However, we don’t want to synchronize all specifications from the S/4HANA system, therefore the synchronization only happens if there are specific specification types set in the Customizing of the respective value assignment types. If there is no specification type set, then the respective property won’t be created on the SAP IPD side.

If there is a specification type set for B and C type value assignment types, then there is another decision to make: how should these items be created on the SAP IPD side?

Let’s go a little deeper into this.

In SAP Integrated Product Development, composition is modelled a little bit differently from SAP S/4HANA specification management. For the simplest of components, that only need a name and maybe translation, SAP IPD introduceda lean object called “composition items”. These always have a composition item type (for example nutrients or allergens), and they can be grouped arbitrarily.

When there is a composition property to synchronize, you as an administrator have to decide how to replicate the possible values of that property. You do that based on specification types, in the Synchronize Data app, with the Configuration for Composition Properties tile.

Here you have to add each specification type that is used as possible value for a B or C type property, and decide if the specifications within that specification type should be replicated as specifications or composition items.

Composition items are simpler objects than specifications, which can be a pro and a con, based on the need of your company.

If you choose composition item, there is no further setting to do.

If you choose specifications, then you’ll need a status profile set for them, and also determine a released status and an in work status for them. Remember that these have to have direct status transition between each other, since the updating of these items happens with the proper statuses.

Important: after setting this in the configuration and doing the first synchronization, the chosen setting in the Configuration for Composition Properties tile is not modifiable anymore. Why? Of course because it would lead to inconsequencies.

When synchronizing a C type composition property, component types also come into the picture. Here the synchronization creates all component types in the S/4HANA system, but creates a role in composition group on the SAP IPD side that will be assigned to the respective property, and it will only contain the component types (here named as roles in composition) that are set in the S/4HANA Customizing (Specify Context-Specific component types) for the given value assignment type. If nothing is set here, then all component types can be used as values, and the same will be set on the SAP IPD side.

VAT Type RMS-D and RMS-N

For RMS-D and RMS-N type properties, the decision is very similar. You need to decide if the specifications that can be the values in the qualitative or quantitative composition should be composition items or specifications on the SAP IPD side. Here however, there is another Customizing taken into consideration: you need to take the specification type from the respective recipe calculation, and make sure that is added to the configuration.

In the case of RMS-D and RMS-N type properties, it is also very important to mention that SAP IPD Formulation uses composition items for calculation, therefore if you plan to use Formulation with Specification management within SAP IPD, then you are recommended to create composition items for qualitative and quantitative composition.

For RMS-D type properties, exception values are also needed. It is handled similarly to component types: all exception values in the S/4HANA system are replicated to the SAP IPD system, and then a qualitative statement group is created for the given property, which only consists of the exception values specified in the customizing Assign Exception Values to Specification Types and Specify Context-Specific Exception Values.

Last but not least, for RMS-N type properties, both component types and exception values are needed, but they are handled in the same way as explained before.

Actual Steps to Take

The functionality is well documented on the Help Portal, please go through the topic and subtopics as well before starting the configuration process.

Integrating with SAP S/4HANA Specification Management | SAP Help Portal

Summary

So before synchronizing property trees from S/4HANA to SAP IPD, please take your time to design and review the above mentioned points, and decide when needed. Then, if you have composition properties, add the relevant specification types to the Configurations for Composition Properties tile. After all of those are ready, you can add the property trees and synchronize your systems.

Watch out for errors and warnings, and resolve them to be able to completely synchronize your property trees.

Remember, that the leading system  is always the S/4HANA system, so modifying replicated objects on the SAP IPD side can lead to inconsistencies and even the impossibility of transferring values, therefore no modification is recommended on them.

About the Author

Zsofia is a development manager at SAP IPD, working in the PLM area for 10+ years.

entry.title: Re: Next Phase of the New Table Build Experience: Switching Existing Stories
entry.content:
Hi @Dominik_Z ,

sorry for the delayed response!

Both statements are correct. the dialog will affect the bahevior in edit mode. So you will migrate your story for edit mode to the new table experience.

In view mode the new experience has been used for most cases for a few quarters now so is not linked to the status in edit mode. This behavior will continue as is.

If you convert your story in edit mode, it will definitely also use the new experience in view mode.

If you do not convert thus you cancel the migration, the story will not use the new table experience in edit mode but it will most likely still use it in view mode.

I hope this answers your question.

Best

Anna

entry.title: Re: EMail not received from CALM
entry.content:
If no alerts are being generated, first refer to the following KBA and make sure the configuration is fully complete:
3436616 - How to configure SAP Cloud ALM Health Monitoring metric thresholds, alerts, and e-mail notifications?

 

The logic to create alerts and chat/e-mail notifications in Health Monitoring is based on the Metric's defined thresholds. 

  1. The alert/event will be first created once the metric value passes at least one threshold that has been specified in the monitoring configurations. If chat/e-mail notifications are configured as well, they will also be sent at this time. The possible transitions are: 
    1. Ok (green) to Warning (yellow)
    2. Ok (green) to Critical (red)
  2. Further chat/e-mail notifications will be sent if there is a change in the event rating. A new alert will not be created in these scenarios, the existing alert will continue to be used.
    1. Warning (yellow) to Critical (red)
    2. Critical (red) to Warning (yellow).
  3. Note: As of now, Cloud ALM does not send a new Alert/Event if it is resolved (from Red/Yellow to Green). 

Please note that a continued Warning (yellow) or Critical (red) status in an alert will not trigger additional notifications about the alert. This can occur if a metric collects data about several different entities of the same metric category, but they all have the same status based on the thresholds.

  • For example, the "Certificates" metric will collect data about expiring certificates in a system. A new alert will be created and chat/e-mail notifications will be sent if at least 1 certificate expiry date falls within the Warning (yellow) threshold specified in the monitoring configurations. If another certificate expiry date falls into the Warning (yellow) threshold value the next day, it will be silently added to the "Alert Details" table of the existing alert, without any chat/e-mail notification. A chat/e-mail notification will only be sent if at least one of the certificate expiry dates pass into the Critical (red) threshold, thus changing the overall alert status to Critical (red) as well.

entry.title: Restrict Maintenance Notification conversion to only Reactive Maintenance Orders
entry.content:
We are trying to restrict Maintenance Notification conversion to only Reactive Maintenance Orders in the Manage Maintenance Notifications app.

We identified that the restriction field AUFART (Order Type) is available in business catalog SAP_EAM_BC_MN_MNG_PC (via fields like AUFART_IWERK, AUFART_WERKS, etc.). However, these same restriction fields are also used in catalog SAP_EAM_BC_MP_ORD_MNG_PC, which is responsible for maintenance order creation.

Our concern is that restricting AUFART at the catalog level would remove order type options not only during notification conversion but also in the “Create Maintenance Order” app, leading to unintended cross-app impacts.

Could you please advise on the best approach in S/4HANA Public Cloud to restrict notification-to-order conversion specifically to Reactive Maintenance, without affecting direct order creation or other apps?

entry.title: Post valuation for real estate contract on multiple ledgers
entry.content:
When I post valuation for a real estate contract it only post against one ledger. Either on the one maintained in the valuation rule or determining ledger from accounting principle (if not maintained in rule) How can the system post valuation on all ledgers without assining multiple rules in the contract?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Post valuation for real estate contract on multiple l...
entry.content:
Thank you for your question!

Valuation rules are always accounting principle and ledger dependent. The valuation rules determine how the valuation for the respective accounting principle is calculated and to which ledgers or accounts the postings are made.

If it is required to post valuation against several ledgers for a lease contract, then at least one valuation rule for each required ledger should be maintained on the contract valuation parameters.

This standard system logic, as well as further important considerations related to valuation postings are outlined in the best practice test script available for the CLM scope items (1T6 linked as an example). It is highly recommended to read through these best practice documentations to learn more about the solution and the standard system logic related to lease contracts.

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Parameters Automatically Mapped as Fro...
entry.content:
Hello 

There is no SAP Business One setting to automatically map Crystal Report parameters.
This usually happens because SAP B1 is reading cached/old parameter metadata or the report was imported with an old parameter structure.

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Sap analytical cloud free trail to more 30 days
entry.content:
Hello @Yash_Singh_Singh 

SAP Analytics Cloud free trial extension is usually not available from the SAC tool itself.
You can try these options:

Check your registered email for any trial extension link from SAP.
Log in to SAP Store / SAP BTP Cockpit and check if extension option is available.
If no option is shown, raise a request with SAP Support / SAP Sales.
Otherwise, create a new trial account using another eligible email ID.

So there is no direct setting inside SAC to extend the free trial manually.

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: WebGUI Short Dump GETWA_NOT_ASSIGNED in CL_HTTP_EXT_I...
entry.content:
Hello @pinnie 


This is most likely user-specific WebGUI/ITS session or personalization corruption.
Not authorization issue. It is likely ITS/WebGUI user-session, personalization, or SAP kernel/ITS bug
Ask Basis team to check, Kill your active sessions in, SM04 / AL08,
Clear HTTP/WebGUI sessions in SMICM

Reset/delete your WebGUI personalization.
Check your user menu/roles for invalid WebGUI entries.
Test by copying your roles to a new user.
If still same, check/apply SAP Notes for.

 

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: EMail not received from CALM
entry.content:
Hello @shivakul54 


Test email works, the issue is not mail server related. It is most likely due to missing subscription, incorrect notification rule, scope/tag mismatch, or user email configuration.


Verify the user is added as recipient/subscriber for the alert/event.
Check if email notification is enabled in SAP Cloud ALM notification settings.
Confirm the correct event type / managed tag / scope is assigned.
Check if alert conditions are actually triggered.
Verify user email address in IAS / SAP Cloud ALM user profile.
Check spam/junk folder and mail rules.

 

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Is that possible to change SAP Cloud ALM alert to oth...
entry.content:
Hello 


In SAP Cloud ALM, alert/email language is generally based on the tenant/user/browser language settings, not directly controlled per alert in Integration & Exception Monitoring.

Change user language in SAP Cloud Identity / user profile.
Change browser language to Japanese (JA).
Log out and log in again to SAP Cloud ALM.
Check if notification/email template supports Japanese.

 


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Additional Email Notification to other recipients aft...

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is currently no standard configuration to send Sales Order approval completion emails to additional arbitrary users based on Sales Office.

 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Charts / KPI Visualization with RAP Custom Entities

There is no fully standard/recommended way to build rich chart/KPI visualizations directly on RAP custom entities. Use CDS analytical views with UI annotations, or build a custom UI extension for custom-entity-based charts.

entry.title: Re: Metadata update has been deferred issue in SSAM

Hello 


This usually happens when the locally bundled metadata is not detected/loaded correctly, so MDK waits for metadata from the server. When you click Now, it downloads metadata from BTP and works.

entry.title: Re: Change of Delivery Class of existing Database tables...
entry.content:
Hi @manichand_ale 

When designing the data model of your ABAP application, you should separate system, configuration, and application data. You can then select the correct delivery class for each table. Delivery class A is intended for application data. So if you use it for a table with configuration data, I think the data model will be inaccurate. An inaccurate data model makes it difficult to maintain the application.

In addition, the delivery class also has technical consequences you need to consider for example during client copy or BC Set deployment: https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/index.htm?file=abenddic_database_tab...

When changing the delivery class specifically from A to C you should not have any data loss. The main impact is about the change in behavior described in above link.

Regards,
Patrick

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Parameters Automatically Mapped as Fro...

entry.title: Re: Anyone else using AI to help build SAP B1 reports?
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm a 20-year-old experienced developer, and I use AI in my daily work. Nothing new — we already had automation before; otherwise, we'd still be scraping bits today.

I've used AI to write SQL queries, but never to build a Crystal Report from scratch until now. Regarding your questions:

  1. Are others using AI for SAP B1 SQL work? What's working, what isn't? - I use to create scripts and to improve existing ones. Like everything AI generated it needs validation after, so I can rewrite some parts of it.
  2. What kinds of reports do you find most painful or time-consuming to build? - The ones with to much sub-details and sub-reports. But that was always like that, even before AI.
  3. How do you handle data-entry inconsistencies that affect report outputs? -  I prefer to handle in the SQL query most of the time. There are some cases when data that needs to be reviewed prior , that can be done by a user or db adjustment routine.

 Best regards,

William Wachholz - https://williamwachholz.vercel.app/

entry.title: Re: GRIR Account sustitution User exit
entry.content:
Hello @leah_huang71 ,

Your issue occurs because GGB1 + user exit substitution runs too late in the posting process. By that time, SAP has already derived OIM behavior, sort key, BSIS currency fields, and clearing logic from the original GR/IR account (431050). To solve this correctly, the account replacement must happen earlier during MM/FI account determination (before FI document enrichment), using an earlier enhancement point or BAdI rather than FI substitution logic.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Change Absolute Tax Exchange Rate when exchange rate...
entry.content:
Hello @yuki_0717 ,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, manually changing the document exchange rate does not always automatically recalculate the absolute tax amount using that manual rate, because the SAP tax engine still follows standard tax determination and currency conversion logic. Standard Public Cloud configuration does not generally provide a simple option to freely override the calculated absolute tax amount during posting. If this is a recurring legal/business requirement, the recommended approach is usually to implement a controlled manual tax adjustment process or review the tax code and posting design rather than relying on manual exchange-rate overrides alone.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

William Wachholz - https://williamwachholz.vercel.app/

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One Down Payment Reconciliation Error
entry.content:
Hello @Thanigai_vel ,

I am not an expert on SAP Business one, commenting based on my knowledge. The error occurs because SAP Business One does not allow a regular Incoming Payment to be directly reconciled with a payment originating from a Down Payment Request. The remaining 7,700 INR should not have been posted as a normal Incoming Payment. The correct approach is to reverse/cancel that payment if possible and process the refund through the proper AP Down Payment adjustment, cancellation, or credit memo process so the document flow remains consistent.

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Which BADI is used to add sold to party bp in conditi...
entry.content:
Perform the below steps

  1. Create a custom logic implementation in the Custom Logic app (or via classic BAdI in on‑premise).
  2. Use the BAdI (APOC_ADD_CONDITION_PARAMETER or APOC_ADD_EMAIL_RECIPIENTS) to read the sold‑to party from the sales document header partners.
  3. Populate this value into the OPD context so it can be used in BRF+ decision tables.
  4. Update your BRF+ decision table to include the new condition column “Sold‑to Party.”
  5. Test by creating a sales order and checking if the output determination now evaluates the sold‑to party.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Dimension Tables Move to the File Repository
entry.content:
Introduction

Dimension tables in SAP Analytics Cloud are moving to the file repository. This completes the journey that started with stories, models, data actions, multi actions and currency conversion tables — and it brings with it a significantly simpler approach to privilege management, a cleaner user experience, and a unified way to organize all your planning artifacts in one place.

In this post we walk through what is changing, what the new experience looks like, and what you need to know about the central migration that will move your existing dimensions automatically. This enhancement is shipped with the QRC3.2026 upgrade on QRC and preview systems and with wave 2026.11 on fast-track tenants.

 

Why This Matters: Key Benefits

All in one place

Dimension tables are the final object type to make the move. With this release, stories, models, data actions, currency conversions, and dimension tables all live in the file repository and follow the same handling paradigm. There is no longer a separate mental model for dimensions — everything works the same way.

Greatly Simplified Privilege Maintenance

Managing access to dimensions has historically been one of the more painful aspects of SAP Analytics Cloud administration. The new approach eliminates that friction in several important ways.

  • There is no longer any need to maintain roles specifically to hide a dimension that contains sensitive data. File repository sharing handles this naturally.
  • When a new dimension is created in "My Files", safe defaults apply automatically: only the creator has full control, plus administrators with access to private user folders. No one else can see the new dimension until it is explicitly shared.
  • You can configure access on a folder once and then simply move dimensions into that folder — they automatically inherit the folder's privileges. This is a significant time saver for tenants with many dimensions and complex access requirements.
  • A long-standing pain point is also resolved: the role maintenance UI used to display every individual dimension, including hundreds or thousands of "Date" and "Version" dimensions. This made it nearly impossible to identify such specific dimensions and, in tenants with large numbers of dimensions, caused the UI to become slow due to missing lazy loading. Individual dimensions no longer appear in the role maintenance UI at all.

 

What Changes in the User Interface

Public Dimensions in the File Repository

Public dimensions are now visible in the file repository browser alongside models, stories, and other artefacts. You can rename them, move them, update their descriptions, or transfer ownership — all without affecting any models that reference them.

Sharing Instead of Role Maintenance

To change privileges for a dimension or grant access to other users or teams, you now use the standard file repository sharing functionality. Individual dimensions no longer appear in the role maintenance UI.

Public Dimension Tables Landing Page

The public dimension table landing page no longer loads and displays all public dimensions on open. Instead, it shows the 25 most recently used public dimension tables — consistent with the behavior already in place for models and currency conversions.

Saving a New Public Dimension

When saving a new public dimension, the file repository save dialog appears, allowing you to specify the parent folder, the name, and a description — exactly as you would when saving any other file repository artifact.

Creating Dimensions Inside a Model

It is no longer possible to choose "Make Public" when adding a new dimension directly inside a model. The alternatives are:

  • Create public dimensions upfront before creating the model and reference them when building the model.
  • Create a new private dimension, save the model, then use "Save As Public Dimension" to create a copy and "Dimension Table > Replace" to swap the Private dimension for the newly saved public one.

New Create Dimension Dialog

The "Create Dimension" dialog has been redesigned. It now includes a file repository browser for selecting existing public dimensions of a given type — Account, Organization, or Generic. In seamless planning models, it only allows picking dimensions saved in the same SAP Datasphere space as the model.

Replace Dimension Dialog

It now includes the same file repository browser for selecting existing public dimensions.

Private Dimension Tables

Private dimension tables do not appear in the file repository browser. However, access management has moved away from roles and uses file repository functionality:

  • You now control who can see and edit a private dimension using the same file repository sharing mechanisms available for all other artifacts, reachable directly from the model or dimension editor. Sharing a model with a user or team automatically shares all private dimensions of that model with the same privileges.
  • It is also possible to restrict access to individual private dimensions further than the model itself.

 

Central Migration: What Happens to Your Existing Dimension Tables

The migration to the file repository runs automatically as part of the QRC3.2026 upgrade on QRC and preview tenants or as part of the wave 2026.11 upgrade on fast-track tenants. Here is what you need to know.

What Gets Migrated

  • All public dimension tables receive a file repository artefact.
  • These artefacts are created in a new folder: Public > Dimension Tables.
  • Private dimensions are technically migrated as well but they will not be visible in the file repository.

What does not change

Note that the migration does not change the dimension tables itself. IDs and content stay the same. No effects on the consumption in models, stories, data actions etc. are to be expected.

How Existing Role Assignments Are Handled

The migration applies the same algorithm used for earlier file repository migrations such as Data Actions, Multi Actions and Currency Conversions.

  • Teams following the naming convention "DimensionRole_<role name>" are generated for roles where at least two users have that role directly assigned. Those users are added to the generated team, and the relevant dimensions are shared to that team. For roles with single user or only team(s), the permissions will be attached to the individual or team(s).
  • If the tenant already has all users organized in teams with roles assigned to those teams, no additional teams will be generated — existing teams are reused.

Privilege gaps after migration

Due to the way file repository privileges work, some users may find they are missing access to dimensions after the migration.

  • Before the migration, a create-only role was sufficient — users automatically had full control over any dimension they created as the owner. The file repository does not work this way.
  • To achieve the same level of control as before, users need a role that explicitly grants Read, Delete, Update, and Maintain privileges.
  • We recommend reviewing your role assignments after the migration and updating them accordingly.

Import of Transport Packages

When importing transport packages created before the migration, dimension tables are automatically saved/migrated to the file repository.

Bookmarks to Public Dimension Tables

Browser bookmarks created to directly access public dimension tables will cease to work after the migration. We need to add a prefix for all public dimension tables to avoid name collisions with other objects.

Behavior During the Migration Window

While the migration is running, creating and deleting dimensions is blocked and will result in backend errors. Depending on the number of dimensions and roles in your tenant, this could take several hours.

The migration runs right after the upgrade of your tenant to QRC3/wave 2026.11. This means users can be logged in while the migration is in progress and may encounter these backend errors during that window.

 

Summary

The move of dimension tables to the file repository is a significant step forward. It completes the unification of all object types under a single, consistent artefact management model, eliminates long-standing pain points in role maintenance, and gives administrators a much more intuitive and scalable way to control access to sensitive data. We recommend reviewing your role assignments after the migration and updating them accordingly.

entry.title: Get Early Insights to SAP AI Innovations: Join SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 by June 19
entry.content:
At SAP Sapphire, SAP reaffirmed its strong commitment to AI-driven innovation and its role in transforming businesses.

Innovation at SAP is built on collaboration. Leveraging cutting-edge technologies like Artificial Intelligence, SAP continues to drive meaningful business value for customers. Now, we invite you to be part of this journey by participating in SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects focused on AI.

🗓Registration is open until June 19, 2026.

In today’s fast-evolving business landscape, early involvement is key. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can collaborate directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing feedback on concepts, prototypes, and innovations while they are still taking shape.

View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the selected exciting opportunities:

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-Assisted Error Remediation for SAP Application Interface Framework
  • AI assisted guidance in the UI theme designer
  • BTP Specific Data Privacy & AI based Data Masking
  • Fulfillment of business needs through Business Adaption Plans and Solution Implementation
  • Partner Perspectives in SAP BTP and AI development

Cloud ERP

  • Accelerate Integrations with AI powered SAP Integration Suite
  • Agentic AI in SAP S/4HANA Procurement (Private Cloud Edition)
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI in SAP GRC for SAP HANA
  • AI-Powered Warranty Verification – Co-Innovate the future of service, repair & claims
  • Intelligent Returns: Process Discovery & Automation Roadmap
  • Modernization Approaches in Purchase Order (SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition)

SAP Globalization

  • Assessing Demand for AI Speech Services in SAP Translation Hub

SAP Concur

  • Concur Invoice - AI-first, Reimagine Invoice Experience

SAP Customer Engagement Initiative meets SAP Integration Experience Feedback

  • Hands-on: AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Mgmt - Provisioning & Setup
  • Hands-on Experience of AI-powered Dispute Mgmt Scenario with SAP Enterprise Service Management
  • Hands-on System Experience of the AI-Assisted Process Modeler in SAP Signavio

entry.title: Re: How to add a new entity to the RAP BO of a customizing table
entry.content:
@internet_kccrm 

Besides the association to the parent you also define an association to the root. D is the entity, C the parent and All the root

"BDEF entity D:
define behavior for I_SmbcTableD alias SmbcTableD ##UNMAPPED_FIELD
lock dependent by _SmbcTableAAll
authorization dependent by _SmbcTableAAll

association _SmbcTableAAll { with draft; }
association _SmbcTableC { with draft; }

"CDS view entity D
define view entity I_SMBCTABLED
  as select from smbc_d
  association [1..1] to I_SmbcTableA_S      as _SmbcTableAAll on  $projection.SingletonID = _SmbcTableAAll.SingletonID
  association        to parent I_SmbcTableC as _SmbcTableC    on  $projection.KeyA = _SmbcTableC.KeyA
                                                              and $projection.KeyC = _SmbcTableC.KeyC
